message:
test(fcp-core): pin OperationIntent + OperationReceipt signable_bytes determinism (flywheel_connectors-zsdwv)

Bead asks for SignableBytes determinism + canonical encoding. fcp-core
has no type literally named SignableBytes; the signable_bytes() method
is implemented on multiple types in operation.rs:
- OperationIntent::signable_bytes (line 177): domain separator
  b"FCP2-INTENT-V1", canonical header, request_object_id,
  capability_token_jti, idempotency_key (option-tagged), planned_at
  (LE u64), planned_by (length-prefixed), lease_seq (option-tagged),
  upstream_idempotency.
- OperationReceipt::signable_bytes (line 276): domain separator
  b"FCP2-RECEIPT-V1", canonical header, request_object_id,
  idempotency_key (option-tagged), outcome_object_ids (count-prefixed),
  resource_object_ids (count-prefixed), usage_metrics (option-tagged).

Inline tests pin determinism + a couple of distinguishing-input cases.
This integration test pins the gaps:

- Domain separators at exact head for both types (regression-loud
  pinning since drift silently invalidates every existing signature)
- Intent and receipt domain separators are distinct (intent signature
  MUST NEVER verify as receipt and vice versa) — substring check too
- Determinism across 3 calls
- signable_bytes is independent of signature field (you can't sign
  over your own signature)
- Per-axis injectivity: every input axis (request_object_id /
  capability_token_jti / planned_at / planned_by / lease_seq /
  idempotency_key) produces different bytes when changed
- Option-tagging encoding: Some vs None changes bytes for every
  Optional field on Intent (idempotency_key / lease_seq /
  upstream_idempotency)
- Length-prefix injectivity on planned_by: shifting bytes from
  planned_by to upstream_idempotency MUST produce different bytes
  (collision prevention)
- Receipt: empty outcome/resource Vec encodes deterministically and
  is distinct from non-empty
- Receipt: outcome_object_ids and resource_object_ids order matters
  (operators MUST sort if they want order-independent signatures —
  the wire form does not sort for them)
- Receipt: idempotency_key Some-vs-None and outcome count change bytes
- Cross-payload separation: header.zone_id flows through the
  canonical-header step for both Intent and Receipt
- signable_bytes extends past the domain separator (sanity check)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
